input.prompt:
I'm upgrading a .NET 9 app to .NET 10. It:
- Reads SslStream.KeyExchangeAlgorithm, SslStream.CipherAlgorithm, SslStream.HashAlgorithm, and their strength properties for TLS connection logging
- Registers a callback on SystemEvents.EventsThreadShutdown for cleanup
- Uses Queryable.MaxBy with an IComparer<TSource> overload
- Has XSLT transforms with XsltSettings.EnableScript = true
What warnings or errors will I get?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about breaking changes and warnings when upgrading from .NET 9 to .NET 10. Let me research the specific APIs they mentioned to identify what changed in .NET 10.

Let me think about what I know about .NET 10 breaking changes for these specific APIs:

1. **SslStream.KeyExchangeAlgorithm, CipherAlgorithm, HashAlgorithm and strength properties** - In .NET 10, these properties on SslStream were obsoleted. They were marked with `[Obsolete]` attributes because TLS 1.3 doesn't expose individual algorithm details in the same way. The properties return default/zero values for TLS 1.3 connections. They were marked as obsolete warnings.


2. **SystemEvents.EventsThreadShutdown** - This event was also obsoleted in .NET 10, likely due to changes in how the Windows event system is handled.
3. **Queryable.MaxBy and MinBy with IComparer overloads** - The LINQ methods had some overloads removed or changed in .NET 10, specifically those accepting an `IComparer` parameter.
4. **XsltSettings.EnableScript** - Script execution in XSLT was disabled by default or removed entirely in .NET 10 for security reasons.

I should check the official .NET 10 breaking changes documentation to confirm these details and make sure I'm giving accurate information.

If you're migrating an app to .NET 10, the breaking changes listed here might affect you. Changes are grouped by technology area, such as ASP.NET Core or Windows Forms.

This article categorizes each breaking change as _binary incompatible_ or _source incompatible_, or as a _behavioral change_:

*   **Binary incompatible** - When run against the new runtime or component, existing binaries may encounter a breaking change in behavior, such as failure to load or execute, and if so, require recompilation.
    
*   **Source incompatible** - When recompiled using the new SDK or component or to target the new runtime, existing source code may require source changes to compile successfully.
    
*   **Behavioral change** - Existing code and binaries may behave differently at runtime. If the new behavior is undesirable, existing code would need to be updated and recompiled.
    

Note

Contents of https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/compatibility/windows-forms/10.0/obsolete-apis:
Some Windows Forms APIs have been marked as obsolete, starting in .NET 10.

## Previous behavior

Previously, the [affected APIs](#affected-apis) could be used without any build warnings.

## New behavior

In .NET 10 and later versions, use of these APIs produces a compile-time warning with a custom diagnostic ID. The use of custom diagnostic IDs allows you to suppress the warnings individually instead of blanket-suppressing all obsoletion warnings.

The following table lists the custom diagnostic IDs and their corresponding warning messages.

Diagnostic ID

Description

Severity

[WFDEV004](/en-us/dotnet/desktop/winforms/wfdev-diagnostics/wfdev004)

[Form.OnClosing(CancelEventArgs)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.form.onclosing#system-windows-forms-form-onclosing\(system-componentmodel-canceleventargs\)), [Form.OnClosed(EventArgs)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.form.onclosed#system-windows-forms-form-onclosed\(system-eventargs\)) and the corresponding events are obsolete. Use [Form.OnFormClosing(FormClosingEventArgs)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.form.onformclosing#system-windows-forms-form-onformclosing\(system-windows-forms-formclosingeventargs\)), [Form.OnFormClosed(FormClosedEventArgs)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.form.onformclosed#system-windows-forms-form-onformclosed\(system-windows-forms-formclosedeventargs\)), [Form.FormClosing](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.form.formclosing#system-windows-forms-form-formclosing) and [Form.FormClosed](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.form.formclosed#system-windows-forms-form-formclosed) instead.

Warning

[WFDEV005](/en-us/dotnet/desktop/winforms/wfdev-diagnostics/wfdev005)

[Clipboard.GetData(String)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.clipboard.getdata#system-windows-clipboard-getdata\(system-string\)) method is obsolete. Use [Clipboard.TryGetData](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.clipboard.trygetdata) methods instead.

Warning

[WFDEV006](/en-us/dotnet/desktop/winforms/wfdev-diagnostics/wfdev006)

[ContextMenu](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.contextmenu), [DataGrid](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.datagrid), [MainMenu](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.mainmenu), [Menu](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.menu), [StatusBar](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.statusbar), [ToolBar](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.toolbar) are obsolete. They're provided for binary compatibility with .NET Framework.

Warning

## Version introduced

.NET 10

## Type of breaking change

These obsoletion warnings can affect [source compatibility](../../categories#source-compatibility).

## Recommended action

*   Follow the specific guidance provided for the each diagnostic ID using the URL link provided on the warning.
*   If necessary, you can suppress the warning using the custom `WFDEVxxx` diagnostic ID value.

## Affected APIs

### WFDEV004

*   [OnClosing(CancelEventArgs)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.form.onclosing#system-windows-forms-form-onclosing\(system-componentmodel-canceleventargs\))
*   [System.Windows.Forms.Form.OnClosed(EventArgs)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.form.onclosed#system-windows-forms-form-onclosed\(system-eventargs\))

### WFDEV005

*   [System.Windows.Clipboard.GetData(String)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.clipboard.getdata#system-windows-clipboard-getdata\(system-string\))

### WFDEV006

*   [System.Windows.Forms.ContextMenu](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.contextmenu)
*   [System.Windows.Forms.DataGrid](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.datagrid)
*   [System.Windows.Forms.MainMenu](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.mainmenu)
*   [System.Windows.Forms.Menu](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.menu)
*   [System.Windows.Forms.StatusBar](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.statusbar)
*   [System.Windows.Forms.ToolBar](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.toolbar)

## See also

*   [Obsolete Windows Forms features in .NET 10+](/en-us/dotnet/desktop/winforms/wfdev-diagnostics/obsoletions-overview)

Contents of https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/compatibility/core-libraries/10.0/obsolete-apis:
Some APIs have been marked as obsolete, starting in .NET 10. This breaking change is specific to APIs that have been marked as obsolete _with a custom diagnostic ID_. Suppressing the default obsoletion diagnostic ID, which is [CS0618](../../../../csharp/language-reference/compiler-messages/cs0618) for the C# compiler, does not suppress the warnings that the compiler generates when these APIs are used.

## Change description

In previous .NET versions, these APIs can be used without any build warning. In .NET 10 and later versions, use of these APIs produces a compile-time warning or error with a custom diagnostic ID. The use of custom diagnostic IDs allows you to suppress the obsoletion warnings individually instead of blanket-suppressing all obsoletion warnings.

The following table lists the custom diagnostic IDs and their corresponding warning messages for obsoleted APIs.

Diagnostic ID

Description

Severity

[SYSLIB0058](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0058)

The `KeyExchangeAlgorithm`, `KeyExchangeStrength`, `CipherAlgorithm`, `CipherAlgorithmStrength`, `HashAlgorithm`, and `HashStrength` properties of [SslStream](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream) are obsolete. Use [NegotiatedCipherSuite](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream.negotiatedciphersuite#system-net-security-sslstream-negotiatedciphersuite) instead.

Warning

[SYSLIB0059](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0059)

[SystemEvents.EventsThreadShutdown](/en-us/dotnet/api/microsoft.win32.systemevents.eventsthreadshutdown#microsoft-win32-systemevents-eventsthreadshutdown) callbacks aren't run before the process exits. Use [AppDomain.ProcessExit](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.appdomain.processexit#system-appdomain-processexit) instead.

Warning

[SYSLIB0060](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0060)

[System.Security.Cryptography.Rfc2898DeriveBytes](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.cryptography.rfc2898derivebytes) constructors are obsolete. Use [Rfc2898DeriveBytes.Pbkdf2](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.cryptography.rfc2898derivebytes.pbkdf2) instead.

Warning

[SYSLIB0061](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0061)

[Queryable.MaxBy<TSource,TKey>(IQueryable<TSource>, Expression<Func<TSource,TKey>>, IComparer<TSource>)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.linq.queryable.maxby#system-linq-queryable-maxby-2\(system-linq-iqueryable\(\(-0\)\)-system-linq-expressions-expression\(\(system-func\(\(-0-1\)\)\)\)-system-collections-generic-icomparer\(\(-0\)\)\)) and [Queryable.MinBy<TSource,TKey>(IQueryable<TSource>, Expression<Func<TSource,TKey>>, IComparer<TSource>)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.linq.queryable.minby#system-linq-queryable-minby-2\(system-linq-iqueryable\(\(-0\)\)-system-linq-expressions-expression\(\(system-func\(\(-0-1\)\)\)\)-system-collections-generic-icomparer\(\(-0\)\)\)) taking an `IComparer<TSource>` are obsolete. Use the new ones that take an `IComparer<TKey>`.

Warning

[SYSLIB0062](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0062)

[XsltSettings.EnableScript](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.xml.xsl.xsltsettings.enablescript#system-xml-xsl-xsltsettings-enablescript) is obsolete.

Warning

## Version introduced

.NET 10

## Type of breaking change

These obsoletions can affect [source compatibility](../../categories#source-compatibility).

## Recommended action

*   Follow the specific guidance provided for the each diagnostic ID using the URL link provided on the warning.
    
*   Warnings or errors for these obsoletions can't be suppressed using the standard diagnostic ID for obsolete types or members; use the custom `SYSLIBxxxx` diagnostic ID value instead.
    

## Affected APIs

### SYSLIB0058

*   [System.Net.Security.SslStream.KeyExchangeAlgorithm](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream.keyexchangealgorithm#system-net-security-sslstream-keyexchangealgorithm)

Contents of https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/compatibility/core-libraries/10.0/obsolete-apis:
Some APIs have been marked as obsolete, starting in .NET 10. This breaking change is specific to APIs that have been marked as obsolete _with a custom diagnostic ID_. Suppressing the default obsoletion diagnostic ID, which is [CS0618](../../../../csharp/language-reference/compiler-messages/cs0618) for the C# compiler, does not suppress the warnings that the compiler generates when these APIs are used.

## Change description

In previous .NET versions, these APIs can be used without any build warning. In .NET 10 and later versions, use of these APIs produces a compile-time warning or error with a custom diagnostic ID. The use of custom diagnostic IDs allows you to suppress the obsoletion warnings individually instead of blanket-suppressing all obsoletion warnings.

The following table lists the custom diagnostic IDs and their corresponding warning messages for obsoleted APIs.

Diagnostic ID

Description

Severity

[SYSLIB0058](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0058)

The `KeyExchangeAlgorithm`, `KeyExchangeStrength`, `CipherAlgorithm`, `CipherAlgorithmStrength`, `HashAlgorithm`, and `HashStrength` properties of [SslStream](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream) are obsolete. Use [NegotiatedCipherSuite](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream.negotiatedciphersuite#system-net-security-sslstream-negotiatedciphersuite) instead.

Warning

[SYSLIB0059](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0059)

[SystemEvents.EventsThreadShutdown](/en-us/dotnet/api/microsoft.win32.systemevents.eventsthreadshutdown#microsoft-win32-systemevents-eventsthreadshutdown) callbacks aren't run before the process exits. Use [AppDomain.ProcessExit](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.appdomain.processexit#system-appdomain-processexit) instead.

Warning

[SYSLIB0060](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0060)

[System.Security.Cryptography.Rfc2898DeriveBytes](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.cryptography.rfc2898derivebytes) constructors are obsolete. Use [Rfc2898DeriveBytes.Pbkdf2](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.cryptography.rfc2898derivebytes.pbkdf2) instead.

Warning

[SYSLIB0061](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0061)

[Queryable.MaxBy<TSource,TKey>(IQueryable<TSource>, Expression<Func<TSource,TKey>>, IComparer<TSource>)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.linq.queryable.maxby#system-linq-queryable-maxby-2\(system-linq-iqueryable\(\(-0\)\)-system-linq-expressions-expression\(\(system-func\(\(-0-1\)\)\)\)-system-collections-generic-icomparer\(\(-0\)\)\)) and [Queryable.MinBy<TSource,TKey>(IQueryable<TSource>, Expression<Func<TSource,TKey>>, IComparer<TSource>)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.linq.queryable.minby#system-linq-queryable-minby-2\(system-linq-iqueryable\(\(-0\)\)-system-linq-expressions-expression\(\(system-func\(\(-0-1\)\)\)\)-system-collections-generic-icomparer\(\(-0\)\)\)) taking an `IComparer<TSource>` are obsolete. Warning

These obsoletions can affect [source compatibility](../../categories#source-compatibility).

data.content:
All four APIs you're using hit new **obsoletion warnings** in .NET 10. Every one is a compile-time **warning** (not error) with a custom diagnostic ID:

| Diagnostic | API | Replacement |
|---|---|---|
| **SYSLIB0058** | `SslStream.KeyExchangeAlgorithm`, `.CipherAlgorithm`, `.HashAlgorithm` and their `*Strength` props | Use `SslStream.NegotiatedCipherSuite` |
| **SYSLIB0059** | `SystemEvents.EventsThreadShutdown` | Use `AppDomain.ProcessExit` |
| **SYSLIB0061** | `Queryable.MaxBy` with `IComparer<TSource>` | Use the new overload taking `IComparer<TKey>` |
| **SYSLIB0062** | `XsltSettings.EnableScript` | XSLT scripting is obsolete; remove script usage |

All are **source-incompatible** warnings (CS0618 won't suppress them — you must suppress each `SYSLIBxxxx` ID individually if needed). None are errors by default, so the build will still succeed with warnings unless you have `<TreatWarningsAsErrors>` enabled.
